msgs.text:
I treated the prompt format as fixed: first two generators are the tame generators sigma,tau, the tame relation is implicit, later generators are in the pro-2 core, and exponents are supplied modulo M=85667662080.The prior log’s only still-live proposal is the dynamic tame-inertia candidate from Turns 9–28; the latest prior gap is explicitly the missing dyadic formation/rigidity step, not another low-level Smith test.

The current live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with π=π2 and η=2π on prime-to-2 tame inertia,r=xσ−πxσπx−1(xτ−η)π ((yτ)π)σy−1,where π≡40491355905,−π≡45176306175,−η=−2π ≡50932630783(modM).Audit: the Turn 28 sign-kernel proof needed a repair There is a real algebraic slip in the body of Turn 28. The conclusion is salvageable, and its final “ESTABLISHED” line states the right formula, but the displayed derivation in the middle inverts the geometric series.

Let Ve,f=kerκ be the sign double cover of the finite-tame pro-2 kernel, and let t be the deck involution. On a nontrivial tame-inertia character block, let τ act by α=1. For a norm wordNa (u)=(uτa)π,the Fox coefficient is the completed geometric seriesDa=j=0∑π−1(tαa)j=1−tαa 1−(tαa)π.

Since π≡1(mod2) and π≡0(mode),(tαa)π=tπαaπ=t,so the correct expression isDa=1−tαa1−t.

This is the direction needed for the proof: 1−tαa is a unit in the coefficient ring because αa=1, so Da∈(1−t), the radical of O[C2]. Hence the correcting factor x−1(xτ−η)π contributes a unit coefficient on every nontrivial tame-inertia character block. Therefore those blocks cannot contribute 2-primary Smith torsion. The Turn 28 conclusion (kerκ)ab≅Z22ef+1⊕C4 still follows, but only with this corrected series.New consequence: all higher cyclotomic branch tests are now formal

The prior turns checked the C4/C8 layer by explicit enumeration in some cases and then by a Demuškin argument. This can be pushed uniformly through the entire 2-power cyclotomic tower.I am using the standard Demuškin facts here: a Demuškin group is characterized cohomologically by one-dimensional H2 and nondegenerate cup product, open subgroups of Demuškin groups are Demuškin, and the q-invariant/orientation records the p-power roots-of-unity behavior in the local Galois case.Numdam Assume the prior finite-tame computation has indeed shown that U=Ue,f is an oriented Demuškin group with d(U)=ef+2,q(U)=2,and with sign class κ. LetU1=kerκ.

Then U1 is the unique index-two subgroup with q=4, andU1ab≅Z22ef+1⊕C4.

For n≥1, define recursively Un+1⊂Un by the orientation character:Un+1=ker(g↦2n+1χ(g)−1 mod2),where q(Un)=2n+1. Then[U:Un]=2n,d(Un)=2n ef+2,q(Un)=2n+1,and consequentlyUnab≅Z22nef+1⊕C2n+1.

Moreover, among the index-two subgroups W<Un, exactly one has q(W)=2n+2 and every other index-two subgroup has q(W)=2n+1.Indeed, the only way an index-two subgroup W=kerψ can have larger q is for χ(W)⊂1+2n+2Z2, i.e. for W to be the kernel of the single nonzero characterλn(g)=2n+1χ(g)−1 mod2.

Thus ψ=λn is forced. All other index-two kernels retain the old q-invariant.

So the Turn 27 distribution2 2ef+2−2 subgroups with C4,1 subgroup with C8 is just the first nontrivial case of a full recursive statement. Once the candidate’s Ue,f is accepted as an oriented Demuškin group with the corrected sign-kernel C4 calculation, every higher C2r branch distribution follows formally. Further enumeration of higher cyclotomic layers should no longer be treated as independent evidence; it is a consequence of the same Demuškin orientation invariant.

What this does and does not prove This turn strengthens the prior state in two ways. First, it repairs the nontrivial tame-character sign-kernel calculation by putting the geometric series in the correct direction. Second, it shows that all higher root-of-unity branch tests are automatic consequences of the oriented Demuškin invariant, not separate obstacles.

But this still does not prove the presentation of GQ2. The essential missing theorem remains:If a profinite group over the tame quotient has, for every finite tame quotient Δe,f, the candidate’s oriented Demuškin kernel together with the exact filtered σπ - and τ−2π -operator data, then it is the arithmetic local Galois formation for Q2.The prior low-depth deformation warnings are still relevant: the abstract Demuškin kernel plus orientation does not by itself determine the full 2-primary Frobenius action. The remaining proof must identify the exact filtered tame action, not merely the cyclotomic torsion pattern.

ESTABLISHED: The Turn 28 sign-kernel proof has a corrected nontrivial tame-character Fox coefficientDa=1−tαa1−t,which does prove unit Smith pivots on all nontrivial tame-inertia blocks. Conditional on the prior finite-tame Demuškin verification, the entire higher 2-power cyclotomic branch distribution follows formally: at each level there is exactly one quadratic branch increasing C2n to C2n+1, and all other branches keep the same torsion order.

GAP: The candidate is still not a proved presentation. The missing step is not another Smith-form or cyclotomic-branch computation; it is the dyadic formation/rigidity theorem identifying the candidate’s exact filtered (σ,τ)-action with arithmetic Frobenius and tame inertia over every finite tame subextension.

NEXT: Formulate the rigidity theorem as a precise comparison of marked oriented Demuškin operator groups: kernel Ue,f, orientation χ, marked S=σf, exact σπ=σsS (1−s)/f, and the tame correction τ−2π. Then test or prove uniqueness of this operator package, rather than continuing isolated abelianization enumerations.[BREAKTHROUGH]
